event: benign prostatic hypertrophy, past medical history
injuring his leg while working on his farm
clinical findings strongly suggestive of C. tetani infection
transferred to our intensive care unit (ICU)
electroencephalography revealed severely slow cerebral activity
tracheal secretions tested positive for Klebsiella pneumoniae and MSSA
transferred to geriatric unit in a coma
breathed spontaneously on 4 L/min of supplemental oxygen via a tracheal cannula
after three days antibiotic therapy was switched to linezolid (14 days)
combined treatment with meropenem for 17 days after septic shock
urinary tract infection caused by K. pneumoniae, Acinetobacter baumannii, Enterococcus faecalis
transferred from geriatric medicine unit to rehabilitation unit
still required tracheal supplemental oxygen (1 L/min)
pressure ulcers on the right (unstageable) and left (stage II) heels, sacrum (stage II), and right elbow (stage III)
AF with a third-degree atrioventricular block (heart rate, 30 beats/min)
transferred to our hospital’s cardiac ICU
hyperkinetic delirium during the postoperative course
treated with ceftazidime-avibactam and amikacin for 1 week
transferred to a geriatric medicine unit
treated with fidaxomicin for ten days
bloodstream infection due to Candida parapsilosis (fluconazole-resistant)
treated with caspofungin and cefazolin for 17 days
bloodstream infection caused by P. aeruginosa
shifted to aztreonam and ceftazidime-avibactam owing to evidence of antibiotic resistance
antibiotic was shifted to cefepime for 10 additional days
tracheostomy closure performed by an ENT specialist and a pulmonologist
nutritional supplementation prescribed to manage malnutrition and sarcopenia
able to perform postural transition with assistance at discharge
motor and respiratory reconditioning continued at discharge
rehabilitation, ENT, and geriatric follow-up evaluations recommended
